2. If we leave right now, we could … catch the train.
1)still
2)while
3)although
2)while

3. Mary is allergic to fish, ...  she sometimes eats it.
1)in summary
2)in addition to
3)neverthless
3)nevertheless

Fill in the gaps with although or in spite of.
 
1. The plate broke,  
 it landed on the carpet.
 
2.  
 she had a flu, she went to work.
 
3.  
 the rain, we went to the park.

1. Although the plate broke, it landed on the carpet.
2. In spite of having a flu, she went to work.
3. Although it was raining, we went to the park.

Fill in the gaps with although or in spite of.
 
1. The plate broke,  ... it landed on the carpet.
 
2.  ... she had a flu, she went to work.
 
3.  ... the rain, we went to the park.

1. Although the plate broke, it landed on the carpet.
2. In spite of her flu, she went to work.
3. Although it was raining, we went to the park.

Join the sentences using although. Write full sentences:
 
1. I hate thrillers. I liked this one.
 
    
 
2. Sarah worked hard. She didn’t pass the test.
 
    
 
3. Sam doesn’t like fast food. Sometimes he can eat some burgers.

1. Although I hate thrillers, I liked this one.
2. Although Sarah worked hard, she didn’t pass the test.
3. Although Sam doesn’t like fast food, sometimes he can eat some burgers.

Rewrite the following sentences using appropriate linking words.
 
  
First, After that, Then/Next, Immediately, While,

The following day, As soon as, In the end,

Meanwhile, Finally, Suddenly, After a while
 
The sentences:
 
I called the police. Dave tried to catch the thief.

We were walking along the beach. We heard somebody shouting.

She saw the flames. She called the fire brigade.

They called for help. A man came and rescued them.

Kate looked everywhere for her keys. She found them under her bed.

We checked into the hotel. We went to our room and unpacked.

He fell fast asleep. He woke up early and drove out to the lake.

The children played in the sea. Their parents relaxed on the beach.

1. I called the police while Dave tried to catch the thief.
2. While we were walking along the beach, we heard somebody shouting.
3. As soon as she saw the flames, she called the fire brigade.
4. After they called for help, a man came and rescued them.
5. Kate looked everywhere for her keys, finally she found them under her bed.
6. First, we checked into the hotel, then we went to our room and unpacked.
7. He fell fast asleep. Suddenly, he woke up early and drove out to the lake.
8. Meanwhile, the children played in the sea and their parents relaxed on the beach.
(Note: For the last sentence, "meanwhile" is used because both actions are happening at the same time)

Choose the linkers and or but:
  
1. Becky finished her article  ... gave it to Harry.
 
2. We saw Jake  ... we didn’t see Sam.
 
3. Sally had lunch ... left for work.

1. and
2. but
3. and

Choose because, and, although, so, but:
  
1.  ... she couldn’t speak Italian, she understood what the man said.
 
2. I have met Kim  ... I haven’t met her brother.
 
3. She didn’t go to the concert  ... she had not bought tickets in advance.

1. Although
2. but
3. because

Choose the correct ending and practise with linkers so and because.
Match the parts of these sentences. Write the numbers:
  
1. I had a party  
 
2. We were bored,  
 
3. He cleaned the room  
 

1) so he was put in prison.

2) because she was cold.

3) so I made a sandwich.

4) because it was my birthday.

5) because I went to bed late last night.

6) because it was a mess.

7) because they were dirty.

8) so the food was burnt.

9) so we went for a walk.

10) so the boss was angry.

11) because her hair was too long.

12) because my old one was broken.

1. 4) because it was my birthday.
2. 9) so we went for a walk.
3. 6) because it was a mess.

Read these sentences and choose only one variant.
 
1. Tina visited the museum yesterday, and her friend  ... went there.
1) either
2) too
3) also
2) too

2. It is a cheap and,  ... , effective way of learning French.
1)unlike
2)moreover
3)in spite of
2)moreover
